⚖️

Inheritance Calculator

Calculate inheritance shares according to Islamic Sharia law

Affects certain cases like grandfather with siblings and al-mushtaraka

1

Estate Data

Bequests cannot exceed one-third of the estate, and are not permitted to an heir without the consent of the other heirs
⚠️
Warning: The entered bequest exceeds one-third of the estate after debts. It has been automatically reduced to the maximum Sharia limit of one-third.
Net Estate 0

Father's Paternal Uncle's Sons
1
ℹ️
Dhawu al-Arham inherit only when no fard heirs (except spouses) or asaba heirs exist. When 'Majority' is selected, the Tanzil method (Hanbali) is used. Maliki and Shafi'i schools do not grant inheritance to distant relatives.
⚠️
Dhawu al-Arham are calculated here using the Tanzil method (Hanbali). The Hanafi school uses the Ahl al-Qaraba method — ranking by class, then degree, then strength of kinship — which may produce different results. Please consult a qualified scholar.
Daughter's Sons

Full Maternal Aunts

Maternal Aunts (Father's side)

Maternal Aunts (Mother's side)
1
Paternal Uncle's Daughters
1
4

Results

⚠️
Note: Awl occurred in this case
Awl means the total fixed shares exceed the estate base, so all shares are proportionally reduced.
ℹ️
Note: Radd occurred in this case
Radd means the total fixed shares don't exhaust the estate and there's no residuary heir, so the remainder is returned proportionally to fixed-share heirs.
📚
This is the al-Akdariyyah case
A famous inheritance case: the grandfather's and sister's shares are combined after awl, then redistributed 2:1 (male gets twice the female share).
🏛️
No fixed-share or residuary heirs found
According to Hanafi and Hanbali schools, the estate goes to distant relatives (dhawu al-arham) such as daughter's children, maternal uncles, and paternal aunts. According to Maliki and Shafi'i schools, it goes to the Muslim public treasury (Bayt al-Mal). Consult a qualified scholar to determine the rightful heirs.
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦
Dhawu al-Arham
Inherits by Tanzil — placed in the position of the person through whom they connect to the deceased
Heir Share Amount Type Explanation

🚫 Blocked Heirs

    Total Distributed 0
    No heirs selected

    ⚠️ Important Notes

    • This calculator is for guidance only and is not a religious ruling (fatwa).
    • It is recommended to consult a qualified Islamic scholar or Sharia court.
    • A deliberate killer and a person of different religion do not inherit.
    • Madhab differences currently implemented: grandfather with siblings (Hanafi), al-Mushtaraka (Maliki/Shafi'i), paternal grandmother blocked by father (Hanafi only), al-Akdariyyah (non-Hanafi). For other madhab-specific rulings, consult a qualified scholar.

    📖 How to Use

    Enter the estate amount, debts, and bequests, then select the deceased's gender, then choose the existing heirs and their numbers. Results will appear automatically showing each heir's share and its Sharia basis.
